title: Eulophia rupincola
taxonomy: Eulophia rupincola is classified within the genus Eulophia, which belongs to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This species is further categorized under the subfamily Epidendroideae. Within the Orchidaceae family, Eulophia rupincola is part of the tribe Cymbidieae. The tribe Cymbidieae is then grouped under the subfamily Epidendroideae. Eulophia rupincola's classification is also often referred to as a terrestrial orchid.

intro: Eulophia rupincola, also known as the "Rupicolous Eulophia," is a species of orchid plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eulophia rupincola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Eulophia rupincola is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to treat various ailments, and it is also used in the production of tinctures and extracts for its medicinal properties.
